title: TunnelBear VPN Review 2026: Free Plan, Cute Bears & Real-World Test Results
metaDescription: Our hands-on TunnelBear review for 2026 covers the free 2GB plan, real speed tests, Cure53 security audits, GhostBear obfuscation, streaming results, and how it compares to NordVPN and Surfshark.

testResults.downloadSpeed: 252 Mbps average (macOS, WireGuard) / 55 Mbps average (Windows, OpenVPN)
testResults.noLogsClaim: Verified by Cure53 annual audits (9 consecutive years, 2017–2025)

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict TunnelBear is the friendliest VPN on the market — literally. The bear-themed branding, playful animations, and genuinely simple interface make it the most approachable VPN for non-technical users. After extensive testing across macOS, Windows, Android, and iOS, it earns a **6.8/10** from us. The industry-leading commitment to annual Cure53 security audits (nine consecutive years) is genuinely impressive, and the free 2GB/month plan lets anyone try it without a credit card. However, inconsistent speeds, unreliable streaming unblocking, limited advanced features, and McAfee's corporate ownership drag it below the top tier. **Best for:** VPN beginners who want a dead-simple, privacy-respecting app, casual users who only need basic browsing protection, and anyone who wants to try a VPN free before committing. **Key strengths:** Nine consecutive Cure53 security audits (the most of any VPN), a genuinely useful free plan with 2GB/month, GhostBear obfuscation for censored networks, VigilantBear kill switch with zero DNS/WebRTC/IPv6 leaks detected, and the most beginner-friendly interface in the industry.
💡 Pro Tip: TunnelBear's free plan gives you 2 GB/month — enough for occasional private browsing or securing public Wi-Fi hotspots. To get the most out of it, only activate TunnelBear when using untrusted networks (coffee shops, airports, hotels). For regular daily use, the 2 GB runs out quickly — consider upgrading or switching to Windscribe (10 GB/month free) or ProtonVPN Free (unlimited data on 3 servers).
**Key weaknesses:** Speeds are inconsistent and significantly slower than competitors, streaming unblocking is unreliable (Netflix failed in our tests), only 5 simultaneous connections, no live chat support, McAfee (US) ownership raises Five Eyes jurisdiction concerns, and free users lost server selection and split tunneling in January 2026. --- ## Speed Test Results Speed is TunnelBear's biggest weakness. Performance varies wildly depending on your platform, protocol, and server distance. On macOS with WireGuard, nearby servers deliver acceptable speeds. On Windows with OpenVPN, the results are genuinely poor. Long-distance connections suffer badly on every platform. ### Our Test Environment - **Base connection:** 500 Mbps fiber (symmetric) - **Testing tool:** Ookla Speedtest CLI, repeated 5x per server - **Protocols:** WireGuard (macOS/iOS), OpenVPN (Windows/Android) - **Date tested:** March 2026 ### Results by Server Location (macOS, WireGuard) | Server Location | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | Speed Retention | |----------------|----------------|---------------|-----------|----------------| | Local (nearest) | 389 | 42 | 18 | 78% | | US - New York | 252 | 24 | 72 | 50% | | US - Los Angeles | 187 | 19 | 95 | 37% | | UK - London | 168 | 21 | 88 | 34% | | Germany - Frankfurt | 195 | 22 | 82 | 39% | | Japan - Tokyo | 82 | 8 | 198 | 16% | **Average across all tested servers (macOS):** 252 Mbps download, 24 Mbps upload with 45% speed retention. These numbers are usable for HD streaming and general browsing, but well below what NordVPN (780 Mbps average) or Surfshark (650 Mbps average) deliver. ### Windows Results Tell a Different Story Because TunnelBear uses OpenVPN (not WireGuard) on Windows, speeds drop dramatically: | Server Location | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | |----------------|----------------|---------------|----------| | Local (nearest) | 118 | 12 | 22 | | US - New York | 55 | 5 | 68 | | UK - London | 43 | 4 | 92 | | Japan - Tokyo | 18 | 2 | 205 | The Windows numbers are genuinely poor. At 55 Mbps on a US server from a 500 Mbps base connection, you are losing almost 90% of your speed. TunnelBear needs to bring WireGuard to Windows — the lack of it is a serious disadvantage in 2026. ### TunnelBear vs NordVPN vs Surfshark: Speed Comparison | Metric | TunnelBear | NordVPN | Surfshark | |--------|-----------|---------|----------| | Avg. Download Speed | 252 Mbps (macOS) | 780 Mbps | 650 Mbps | | Avg. Upload Speed | 24 Mbps | 698 Mbps | 590 Mbps | | Speed Retention | 45% | 87% | 79% | | Best Protocol | WireGuard (macOS only) | NordLynx | WireGuard | | Lowest Monthly Price | $3.33/mo | $3.39/mo | $1.99/mo | TunnelBear is significantly slower than every major competitor. The upload speeds are especially weak — 24 Mbps on macOS and 5 Mbps on Windows make video calls, file uploads, and torrenting painfully slow. --- ## Security & Privacy Security is where TunnelBear genuinely shines. No other VPN has committed to as many consecutive independent audits. Nine years of Cure53 audits is an industry record, and it demonstrates a level of transparency that even NordVPN and ExpressVPN have not matched consistently. ### Encryption & Protocols - **AES-256 encryption** across all platforms with SHA-256 authentication - **WireGuard** — available on macOS and iOS only. Fast and modern. - **OpenVPN** — used on Windows and Android. Slower but battle-tested. - **IKEv2/IPsec** — available on Windows and macOS as a fallback. - **Key exchange:** 2048-bit Diffie-Hellman (Windows), 3072-bit (macOS/iOS), 4096-bit (Android) The encryption is strong, but the protocol situation is frustrating. In 2026, not having WireGuard on Windows and Android is a real problem. OpenVPN is secure but significantly slower, and it is the primary reason TunnelBear's Windows speeds are so poor. ### Cure53 Security Audits: The Gold Standard TunnelBear's audit track record is genuinely impressive: - **2017:** First VPN to undergo a public third-party security audit - **2018–2023:** Annual audits continued without interruption - **2024 (8th audit):** Cure53 conducted a white-box assessment over 44 working days with 8 senior auditors. They had full access to source code, server configurations, public-facing APIs, and data handling systems. Found 10 medium-or-higher severity vulnerabilities, 3 low-severity issues, and made 10 minor improvement recommendations. All issues were addressed or mitigated. - **2025 (9th audit):** Completed, results being reviewed and implemented. No other VPN has nine consecutive public audits from the same independent firm. NordVPN has six Deloitte audits. ExpressVPN has multiple KPMG and Cure53 audits. But TunnelBear was the first, and they have never skipped a year. This matters because security is not a one-time achievement — vulnerabilities emerge as code evolves, and annual auditing catches them. ### The McAfee Ownership Question TunnelBear was founded in Toronto, Canada in 2011 by Ryan Dochuk and Daniel Kaldor. In March 2018, McAfee acquired TunnelBear for an undisclosed amount. This raises legitimate concerns: - **Five Eyes jurisdiction:** TunnelBear is a Canadian company owned by an American parent (McAfee). Both Canada and the United States are members of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance. Governments in these countries can compel companies to provide user data. - **Corporate data practices:** McAfee is a massive cybersecurity corporation with a very different culture than a small privacy-focused startup. Corporate priorities can shift. - **Mitigating factor:** TunnelBear's no-logs policy has been independently verified by Cure53 nine times. If they truly log nothing, there is nothing to hand over regardless of jurisdiction. The TunnelBear team has also continued to operate semi-independently within McAfee. Is it ideal? No. Mullvad (Sweden) and ProtonVPN (Switzerland) have cleaner jurisdictional stories. But the audit record provides meaningful reassurance. ### Security Features - **VigilantBear (Kill Switch):** Available on Windows, macOS, and Android. Blocks all internet traffic if the VPN connection drops. In our testing, VigilantBear activated within 2 seconds of a simulated connection drop — no IP leaks detected. - **DNS Leak Protection:** We ran DNS leak tests on macOS, Windows, and Android. Zero leaks detected. TunnelBear operates its own encrypted DNS servers. - **WebRTC Leak Protection:** Tested and confirmed — no WebRTC leaks on any platform. - **IPv6 Leak Protection:** Confirmed — IPv6 traffic is blocked while connected. - **GhostBear (Obfuscation):** Makes VPN traffic look like regular HTTPS traffic using obfsproxy. Available on Windows, macOS (v4), and Android only. Not available on iOS. Useful for bypassing VPN blocks on restrictive networks, corporate firewalls, and in censored countries. Adds some latency — expect 5+ seconds of extra page load time. - **SplitBear (Split Tunneling):** Route only selected apps through the VPN. Available on Android only. Free users lost access to this feature in January 2026. ### No-Logs Policy TunnelBear states it does not log: - IP addresses - DNS queries - Traffic content or destinations - Connection timestamps - Session duration TunnelBear does collect minimal operational data: OS version, app version, total bandwidth used (for free tier enforcement), and whether a connection was made in the last month (active/inactive). This is more than Mullvad collects but standard for commercial VPNs and verified by Cure53. --- ## Streaming Performance Streaming is not TunnelBear's strength. In our March 2026 tests, results were disappointing and inconsistent. If streaming is your primary use case, look elsewhere. ### Platform-by-Platform Results | Platform | Unblocked? | Notes | |----------|-----------|-------| | Netflix US | No | Detected and blocked on all tested US servers. | | Netflix UK | No | Proxy error on London servers. | | Netflix Canada | No | Blocked on Canadian servers. | | BBC iPlayer | No | "Not available in your area" error. | | Disney+ | No | Detected VPN, content region-locked. | | Amazon Prime Video | No | Streaming proxy error on US servers. | | Hulu | No | Blocked. | | YouTube (geo-restricted) | Yes | Successfully accessed region-locked YouTube content. | | Crunchyroll | Partial | Worked intermittently on US servers. | We tested multiple server locations per platform, trying both standard connections and GhostBear mode. The results were consistently poor. Some independent reviewers report occasional success with Netflix US, but in our hands-on testing, we could not achieve reliable access to any major streaming platform. TunnelBear does not market itself as a streaming VPN, and it shows. If you need to unblock Netflix, Disney+, or BBC iPlayer, NordVPN, ExpressVPN, or Surfshark are dramatically better choices. --- ## Pricing & Plans TunnelBear offers three tiers: a free plan, individual paid plans, and a business plan. The free plan is genuinely useful for light users — no credit card required. ### Pricing Overview | Plan | Price | Data | Devices | |------|-------|------|---------| | **Free** | $0 | 2 GB/month | Unlimited (with limitations) | | **Monthly** | $9.99/mo | Unlimited | 5 simultaneous | | **1-Year** | $4.99/mo (billed $59.88/year) | Unlimited | 5 simultaneous | | **3-Year** | $3.33/mo (billed $120/3 years) | Unlimited | 5 simultaneous | | **Teams** | $5.75/user/mo | Unlimited | Per-user management | ### The Free Plan: Genuinely Useful but Increasingly Limited TunnelBear's free plan gives you 2GB of data per month with full encryption and access to all server countries. That is enough for approximately 2-3 hours of HD streaming or a few days of casual browsing. No credit card required — just an email address. However, in January 2026, TunnelBear restricted the free plan significantly: - **Removed:** Custom server selection (you get auto-assigned a server) - **Removed:** SplitBear (split tunneling) - **Kept:** Full encryption, VigilantBear kill switch, all 47 countries These changes make the free plan less flexible but still functional for basic privacy protection when you need it occasionally — at airports, coffee shops, or on public Wi-Fi. ### How TunnelBear Compares on Price | VPN | Cheapest Plan | Data | Connections | Free Tier | |-----|--------------|------|-------------|----------| | TunnelBear | $3.33/mo (3-year) | Unlimited | 5 | Yes (2GB/mo) | | Surfshark | $1.99/mo (2-year) | Unlimited | Unlimited | No | | NordVPN | $3.39/mo (2-year) | Unlimited | 10 | No | | ProtonVPN | $3.59/mo (2-year) | Unlimited | 10 | Yes (unlimited, 5 countries) | | Mullvad | $5.46/mo (flat) | Unlimited | 5 | No | At $3.33/month, TunnelBear is competitively priced but offers less value than alternatives. Surfshark gives you unlimited devices for less money. NordVPN gives you double the connections and vastly better speeds for $0.06 more per month. ProtonVPN's free tier is objectively better — unlimited data on 5 countries versus TunnelBear's 2GB cap. ### Money-Back Guarantee TunnelBear does not offer a traditional money-back guarantee. Refunds are handled on a case-by-case basis through their support team. This is a notable disadvantage compared to the standard 30-day guarantees offered by NordVPN, ExpressVPN, Surfshark, and most competitors. ### Payment Methods Credit card, debit card. No PayPal, no cryptocurrency. The lack of anonymous payment options is a miss for a privacy-focused VPN. ---
💡 Pro Tip: TunnelBear's GhostBear feature disguises VPN traffic to bypass censorship in restrictive countries. Enable it in Settings → GhostBear before traveling to China, Russia, or Iran. Note: GhostBear is only available on paid plans. It makes VPN detection significantly harder but adds 15-30% speed overhead — disable it when you are not in a restricted environment to maintain faster speeds.
## Apps & Usability This is where TunnelBear earns its reputation. The apps are delightful — genuinely fun to use, with bear-themed animations and a world map interface that shows a bear tunneling to your selected country. For VPN beginners, TunnelBear is the least intimidating option available. ### Supported Platforms - **Desktop:** Windows, macOS - **Mobile:** iOS, Android - **Linux:** Manual configuration only (no native app) - **Browser Extensions:** Chrome, Firefox, Opera - **Routers:** Not natively supported - **Smart TV / Streaming Devices:** Not supported The lack of a native Linux app, router support, and smart TV apps is limiting. NordVPN and Surfshark offer all of these. If you need to protect your entire home network from the router level, TunnelBear cannot help. ### Simultaneous Connections TunnelBear allows **5 simultaneous device connections** on paid plans. This matches Mullvad but falls behind NordVPN (10) and Surfshark (unlimited). For a single user, 5 is workable. For a household, it is not enough. ### User Interface The desktop and mobile apps share the same design philosophy: a world map with bear illustrations, one-click connect, and minimal settings. Server selection is country-level only — you pick a country, and TunnelBear auto-assigns the fastest server. City-level selection is available in select countries for paid users but was removed for free users in January 2026. Settings are minimal by design: toggle VigilantBear (kill switch), toggle GhostBear (obfuscation), and that is essentially it. There are no protocol selection options exposed to the user — TunnelBear chooses the protocol based on your platform. Power users will find this frustrating. Beginners will find it perfect. ### Server Infrastructure TunnelBear operates **8,000+ servers across 47 countries**. The server count is competitive — more than Mullvad (approximately 800) but fewer than NordVPN (9,000+) or CyberGhost (11,000+). Country coverage at 47 is adequate but below the 60-100+ offered by larger providers. --- ## What We Do Not Like 1. **Inconsistent, slow speeds.** TunnelBear is one of the slowest VPNs we have tested. Performance varies wildly by platform and server, and the lack of WireGuard on Windows is inexcusable in 2026. 2. **Streaming unblocking fails.** Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Amazon Prime Video — all blocked in our tests. If streaming is your goal, TunnelBear is the wrong choice. 3. **Only 5 simultaneous connections.** NordVPN offers 10, Surfshark offers unlimited. Five is barely enough for a single person with a phone, laptop, and tablet. 4. **No money-back guarantee.** While most VPNs offer a clear 30-day refund policy, TunnelBear handles refunds on a case-by-case basis. This makes trying the paid plan feel risky. 5. **McAfee ownership and Five Eyes jurisdiction.** Canada (TunnelBear) and the United States (McAfee) are both Five Eyes members. The audits mitigate this, but the corporate ownership structure is a legitimate concern. 6. **Free plan restrictions tightened in 2026.** Removing server selection and split tunneling from free users reduces the already limited free tier's usefulness. 7. **No live chat support.** Email only. In 2026, when NordVPN and ExpressVPN offer 24/7 live chat, email-only support feels dated. 8. **No native Linux or router support.** Manual configuration only on Linux and no router support at all significantly limits the platforms TunnelBear can protect. --- ## Who Should Use TunnelBear? ### Best For - **Complete VPN beginners** — If you have never used a VPN and the idea of configuring one feels overwhelming, TunnelBear's one-click interface and playful design removes all intimidation. It is the best first VPN. - **Casual, occasional VPN users** — Need to protect yourself on public Wi-Fi at the airport or coffee shop? The free 2GB plan handles that without spending a cent. - **Privacy-conscious users who value audits** — Nine consecutive Cure53 audits is an industry record. If third-party verification matters more to you than raw speed, TunnelBear delivers. - **Users in mildly restrictive networks** — GhostBear can bypass corporate firewalls and school networks that block standard VPN connections. For heavy censorship (China's Great Firewall), results are less reliable. - **Users who want a no-commitment trial** — The free plan requires only an email address. No credit card, no cancellation hassles. ### Not Ideal For - **Streaming enthusiasts** — TunnelBear failed to unblock Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, and Amazon Prime Video in our tests. Use NordVPN or Surfshark instead. - **Speed-priority users** — At 252 Mbps average (macOS) and 55 Mbps (Windows), TunnelBear is dramatically slower than NordVPN (780 Mbps) or Surfshark (650 Mbps). - **Large households** — Five simultaneous connections is not enough for a family. Surfshark's unlimited connections or NordVPN's 10 are better fits. - **Heavy torrenters** — Speeds are too slow for large file downloads, P2P is limited to select servers, and there are no dedicated P2P-optimized servers. - **Power users** — No protocol selection, no router support, no advanced settings. If you want granular control, Mullvad or ProtonVPN are better choices. - **Linux users** — No native app. Manual configuration only. --- ## Frequently Asked Questions ### Is TunnelBear safe to use in 2026? Yes. TunnelBear is one of the most transparently audited VPNs available. It has completed nine consecutive annual security audits by Cure53, a respected German security firm. The most recent published audit (2024) involved 8 senior auditors over 44 working days with full source code access. Encryption is AES-256, and our leak tests detected zero DNS, WebRTC, or IPv6 leaks. The main concern is McAfee's ownership placing it under Five Eyes jurisdiction, but the audit record provides strong reassurance that no user data is logged. ### Is TunnelBear's free plan worth it? For occasional use, yes. The free plan gives you 2GB of data per month with full AES-256 encryption and access to servers in all 47 countries. That is enough for a few hours of protected browsing on public Wi-Fi. However, since January 2026, free users lost custom server selection and split tunneling. If you need a free VPN for daily use, ProtonVPN's free tier is better — it offers unlimited data on servers in 5 countries. ### Does TunnelBear work with Netflix? In our March 2026 testing, no. TunnelBear was detected and blocked by Netflix on every US, UK, and Canadian server we tried. Some reviewers report occasional success, but we could not achieve reliable access. If streaming is your priority, NordVPN, ExpressVPN, or Surfshark are significantly more reliable choices. ### What is GhostBear and does it work in China? GhostBear is TunnelBear's obfuscation feature that disguises VPN traffic as regular HTTPS traffic using obfsproxy technology. It is designed to bypass VPN detection on restrictive networks. GhostBear is available on Windows, macOS (v4), and Android — but not iOS. For corporate firewalls and school networks, it works well. For China's Great Firewall, results are unreliable — dedicated tools like VyprVPN's Chameleon protocol or Astrill VPN have stronger track records in China specifically. ### How many devices can I connect with TunnelBear? Paid plans allow up to 5 simultaneous device connections. The free plan also supports multiple devices but is capped at 2GB total data per month (shared across all devices). There is no way to increase the device limit — unlike Surfshark (unlimited) or NordVPN (10 devices). If you need more connections, you would need a second account or a VPN with higher limits. ### Who owns TunnelBear? TunnelBear was founded in Toronto, Canada in 2011 by Ryan Dochuk and Daniel Kaldor. In March 2018, McAfee acquired TunnelBear for an undisclosed amount. TunnelBear continues to operate as a semi-independent team within McAfee, and the original development team remained. The company is headquartered in Toronto but falls under McAfee's US parent company, placing it within Five Eyes intelligence alliance jurisdiction. The annual Cure53 audits provide transparency into how user data is (not) handled despite the corporate ownership.
